name: Genetic Algorithm
key_facts: Genetic Algorithms are population-based metaheuristic optimization methods inspired by natural selection
Key operations include selection, crossover (recombination), and mutation
Solutions are encoded as chromosomes (bit strings, real-valued vectors, or other representations)
Fitness function evaluates the quality of each individual in the population
Selection methods include tournament selection, roulette wheel selection, and rank-based selection
Genetic Algorithms were popularized by John Holland in the 1970s
They are derivative-free and can handle discrete, continuous, and mixed optimization problems
forbidden_claims: Genetic Algorithms always converge to the global optimum
Genetic Algorithms are only applicable to binary-encoded problems
Genetic Algorithms were invented by Charles Darwin
